message:
fix(transport,service,report): fix connection side discrimination and harden error propagation

Transport (memory transport):
- Refactor to explicitly track connection sides (A/B) using generation
  parity in handle encoding, eliminating heuristic ring selection that
  caused bidirectional data mixing between client and server
- Clarify mem_read/mem_write routing based on connection side with
  proper parameter validation before ring buffer access
- Implement one-sided close semantics: closing one side does not force
  closure of the other until both are closed

Service (buffered middleware):
- Change buffered service calls to propagate errors instead of silently
  dropping failed invocations, fixing potential data loss in layered
  service stacks

Report:
- Add defensive buffer length clamping in report_buf_append to prevent
  overflows from corrupted or oversized state

Tests:
- Add tests for invalid buffer state, buffered call failure propagation,
  client read isolation across connection sides, and asymmetric closure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
